title: artemisia umbelliformis subsp. umbelliformis
intro: Artemisia umbelliformis subsp. umbelliformis, also known as Alpine Wormwood or Mountain Mugwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. Alpine Wormwood can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
